name: NIHR BioResource
description: The NIHR BioResource is a national research infrastructure of over 200,000 consented volunteers who can be recalled for participation in new studies. It provides genomic data, linked health records, and biomarker data, with the unique capability to re-contact participants for additional data collection, sample provision, or clinical studies.

requirements.name: Data Access Committee approval
requirements.description: Application reviewed by the NIHR BioResource Data Access Committee for scientific merit and governance

requirements.name: Ethics approval
requirements.description: Appropriate research ethics approval for the proposed study

requirements.name: Data access agreement
requirements.description: Formal agreement governing data use, storage, and return/destruction requirements

access_steps.name: Submit research application
access_steps.description: Apply to the NIHR BioResource with research proposal, data/sample requirements, and recall needs

access_steps.name: DAC and ethics review
access_steps.description: Application reviewed by Data Access Committee for scientific merit; ethics approval confirmed

access_steps.name: Data access agreement
access_steps.description: Negotiate and sign data access agreement with NIHR BioResource

access_steps.name: Data/sample provision
access_steps.description: Approved data and/or samples provided to the research team

timeline.notes: Data and/or sample preparation and delivery

costs.notes: Free for NIHR-funded studies. Other funders may incur access or sample processing fees. Recall-for-study costs vary by study requirements.

strengths: Unique recall-for-study capability - can re-contact participants for new research
Active, consented volunteer cohort willing to participate in studies
Genomic data (whole genome sequencing) linked to health records
Rapid recruitment for clinical studies via recall
limitations: Volunteer cohort - not population-representative (healthy volunteer bias)
Smaller than population-level resources (200K+ vs millions)
Recall-for-study adds complexity and cost for studies needing new data collection
recent_changes: Enhanced linked data including primary care records
